mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near historic lows (0.24% as of Dec 2015) keeps the equity risk premium compressed relative to bond yields, sustaining a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as investors continue to rotate from fixed income into equities.
regime_hint: any regime with near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-rate periods show equal or higher forward returns than near-zero periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window from its current 5.0% level, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration, which historically precedes equity market drawdowns as forward earnings expectations are revised down over the next month.
failure_condition: In backtest, triggered episodes produce positive or flat 20d forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the trigger fires fewer than 5 times over a 15-year window making the sample too sparse to test.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that feeds into corporate revenue growth with a lag, producing above-trend S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; neither series alone is sufficient because retail sales can be inflated by price effects while housing starts confirm durable-goods demand.
failure_condition: Joint positive 3-month momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce forward 60d SPX returns exceeding the unconditional sample mean by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) across a 10-year rolling backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During an expansionary industrial cycle, sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect improving corporate operating leverage and capacity utilization, which support positive equity returns over the following quarter, but this relationship inverts or weakens once the cycle matures and margins compress.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum periods in late-cycle regimes (identified by UNRATE below cycle-low thresholds) show forward 60d SPX returns not significantly different from zero or negative, contradicting the expansion-only mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month positive growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a real-time proxy for aggregate income generation and consumer spending capacity, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week as markets price in incrementally improved earnings visibility.
regime_hint: any regime with positive payroll growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS growth and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth accelerates above a threshold consistent with rising inflation expectations, markets anticipate faster Federal Reserve tightening, causing an immediate negative repricing of equity valuations over the following trading week.
regime_hint: rising inflation regime ahead of policy tightening
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns following CPI YoY prints above 2.5% are positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 15-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-cycle upswing, sustained increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal robust household formation and construction-linked demand, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month; this mechanism is muted or reversed during housing downturns when starts decline despite low rates.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns conditioned on rising HOUST momentum show no significant difference from unconditional returns (p > 0.10) across a 10-year rolling backtest, or the relationship is negative during periods of rising HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating industrial production growth (INDPRO) together with a simultaneous uptick in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signals an emerging supply-side slowdown feeding into labor market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; neither indicator alone reliably captures this turning-point dynamic because INDPRO is volatile and UNRATE is a lagging confirmatory signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns negative while UNRATE rises concurrently
failure_condition: Joint trigger episodes (negative INDPRO growth plus rising UNRATE) produce forward 60d SPX returns that are positive or statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional mean in more than 50% of historical occurrences.
